_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch — statewide execs)
_meta.note: Statewide-exec batch (state tier): McNally (TN LtGov/Senate Speaker), Buckingham (TX Land Comm), Miller (TX Ag Comm), Henderson (UT LtGov), Brown (UT AG). Evidence from roll calls, sponsorships, issue-group ratings (TRTL, NRA, GOA), AG amicus briefs/litigation, and direct quotes. CURRENCY FIXES: McNally announced 2026-02-26 he will NOT seek reelection (retiring) -> candidacy_status not_running. Sid Miller LOST the 2026-03-03 GOP primary to Nate Sheets -> status/candidacy lost (leaves active pool). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Deidre Henderson (R), as Utah's chief election officer, publicly defends universal vote-by-mail + machine counting and rebukes her own party's fraud narratives (election_integrity FALSEs cited), and supported SB296 adding SOGI protected classes (biblical_marriage FALSE). Cells with no evidence left null; never inferred from party.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — St. Mary Catholic parish, Oak Ridge
profile.background: Lt. Governor of Tennessee & Speaker of the TN Senate (since Jan 2017). In the General Assembly ~47 years (TN House from 1978; TN Senate from 1986). Hospital pharmacist by profession; longtime Senate Finance chair.

randy-mcnally.notes_set: TN Lt. Governor / Senate Speaker (R). [2026-06-17 refinement, state tier] CURRENCY FIX: on 2026-02-26 (age 82) announced he will NOT seek reelection in Nov 2026, citing his and his wife's health — finishes term to ~Jan 2027; candidacy_status set not_running. Uniformly conservative record: TN Right to Life-endorsed and Senate sponsor of the Human Life Protection Act trigger ban (pro-life); affirms man-woman marriage and backed the 2023 Adult Entertainment Act (drag restriction); supported the 2025 Education Freedom Act universal voucher; backed the 2026 Ten Commandments-in-schools + prayer/Bible-reading bill; decade as Senate Finance chair over TN's balanced budgets/AAA rating; pro photo-voter-ID and Zuckerbucks-ban (under his Senate leadership); Senate sponsor of 2022 Truth in Sentencing; called the Biden vaccine mandate 'the height of federal overreach' and led the counter special session; announced the 2026 immigration-enforcement agenda; NRA-endorsed permitless-carry supporter. Note: softened verbally on same-sex marriage ('abide by Obergefell') but stated policy stayed one-man-one-woman; a 2023 personal-conduct (Instagram) controversy is not a policy vote. Dimensions without a cited individual record left null.

0.note: TN Right to Life-endorsed; pro-life statement on Dobbs ('a great day for the cause of Life').

1.note: Senate sponsor of the Human Life Protection Act trigger ban — a near-total ban, not merely a restriction; presided over its passage.

0.note: Stated position: marriage is the union of one man and one woman; 'strongly disagree' with gender-identity protections.

2.note: As Speaker supported the 2023 Adult Entertainment Act restricting 'male or female impersonator' performances; affirms biological sex.

0.note: Backed the 2025 Education Freedom Act universal voucher; quote favoring parents' freedom to choose schools.

3.note: Backed the 2026 bill mandating Ten Commandments displays plus daily prayer/Bible-reading periods and school chaplains.

2.note: Decade as Senate Finance chair; oversaw TN's constitutionally balanced budgets and AAA credit rating; self-described fiscal conservative.

1.note: Supports photo voter ID ('strongly disagree' with voting without photo ID).

3.note: Supports making ballot harvesting a felony and stricter absentee controls (TN limits absentee to 14 qualifying conditions).

4.note: TN's 2021 'Zuckerbucks' ban (SB1534) barring private election funding passed under his Senate leadership.

2.note: Senate sponsor of the 2022 Truth in Sentencing law (100% time served for certain violent offenses); quote on justice/transparency for victims.

1.note: His office called the Biden employer vaccine mandate 'the height of federal overreach'; led the 2021 counter special session.

3.note: Affirmed state sovereignty against the federal vaccine mandate.

0.note: Stated position: U.S. should physically secure the southern border.

2.note: Announced the 2026 immigration-enforcement agenda; TN's 2025 anti-sanctuary felony law passed under his Senate leadership.

0.note: Supported and voted for 2021 permitless/constitutional carry; quote that it 'reinforces the Second Amendment.'

1.note: NRA-endorsed; stated needed gun restrictions = 'none except to harshly punish criminals with guns.'

profile.religion: Christian (Baptist Ministers Association of Houston endorsement; specific congregation not confirmed)
profile.background: Texas Land Commissioner (General Land Office) since Jan 2023 — first woman in the office. Oculoplastic surgeon (MD, UT Medical Branch Galveston). Former Texas State Senator, SD-24, 2017-2023. ACU lifetime 96%.

dawn-buckingham.notes_set: Texas Land Commissioner (R), since Jan 2023; prior TX State Senator SD-24 2017-2023. [2026-06-17 refinement, state tier] Won the 2026 GOP primary unopposed; faces a Democrat in the Nov 2026 general. Uniformly conservative: Texas Right to Life 100% (86th Leg.) and a sponsor of the 2021 Heartbeat Act (SB8); sponsor of SB29 (2021) biological-sex school sports; conference-committee member on the SB1 (2021) election-integrity omnibus and sponsor of a 'faithless elector' penalty bill; as Land Commissioner offered 1,402 acres of state land for federal deportation facilities and authorized a state border-wall easement (Operation Lone Star); voted YES on 2021 constitutional carry (HB1927), NRA- and Gun Owners of America-endorsed. NOTE: the Permanent School Fund / BlackRock ESG divestment is Comptroller/SBOE-driven, NOT a Buckingham action — not scored. Dimensions without a cited record (school choice, religious liberty, law enforcement) left null.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 100% (35/35), 86th Legislature — supported born-alive and no-taxpayer-funding bills.

1.note: Sponsor/coauthor of SB8 (2021), the Texas Heartbeat Act (~6-week ban) — passed Senate 19-12.

2.note: Sponsor of SB29 (2021) requiring K-12 athletes to compete by biological sex on birth certificate; passed Senate 18-12.

1.note: Conference-committee member on SB1 (2021) election-integrity omnibus, which included voter-ID provisions.

3.note: SB1 (2021) restricted mail-ballot drop boxes and harvesting; she served on its conference committee. Also sponsored a faithless-elector penalty bill.

0.note: As Land Commissioner authorized a 1.45-mile easement for state border-wall construction; coordinated Operation Lone Star border security.

1.note: Offered 1,402 acres of state land for federal deportation facilities to aid removal of illegal aliens.

0.note: Voted YES on HB1927 (2021) constitutional/permitless carry (Senate 18-13 party-line).

1.note: NRA-endorsed (cited ~two dozen pro-gun bills); Gun Owners of America-endorsed as an 'outspoken supporter of Constitutional Carry.'

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian — member and elder, Erath County Cowboy Church
profile.background: Texas Agriculture Commissioner since Jan 2015 (term runs to ~Jan 2027). Former Texas State Representative, HD-59, 2001-2013. Rancher / nursery owner; nine-time rodeo world champion.

sid-miller.notes_set: Texas Agriculture Commissioner (R), since 2015; prior TX State Rep HD-59 2001-2013. [2026-06-17 refinement, state tier] CURRENCY FIX: LOST the 2026-03-03 GOP primary to Nate Sheets (~52-47); not the 2026 nominee — status/candidacy set lost (he serves out his term to ~Jan 2027). Documented conservative positions: primary author of the 2011 pre-abortion sonogram bill (HB15), Texas Right to Life 162% / 2011 Legislator of the Year; rejects transgender ideology (backed the trans military ban; imposed a 'biological gender' TDA dress code); border-security hawk (urged razor wire/drones, 'rattlesnakes' refugee post). DEVIATION FLAG (noted, not scored): as a freshman he was in the TX House that passed HB1403, the 2001 Texas DREAM Act in-state-tuition-for-undocumented law, 142-1 — his individual yea is inferred from the lopsided tally, not a named recorded vote, so left unscored. Most other dimensions (school choice, religious-liberty policy, economics, election mechanics, law enforcement, 2A rating) returned no cited position — left null.

0.note: Primary author of the 2011 pre-abortion sonogram bill (HB15); Texas Right to Life 162% and 2011 Legislator of the Year.

2.note: Imposed a TDA dress code requiring dress 'consistent with biological gender'; backed the trans military ban ('not the place for leftist social experimentation').

4.note: Adopted an anti-LGBTQ employee dress-code policy in his department.

0.note: Urged a stronger physical/active border presence (razor wire, surveillance aircraft/drones), faulting Abbott as too slow.

profile.background: Lieutenant Governor of Utah since Jan 2021 (serving under Gov. Spencer Cox; re-elected 2024). As Lt. Gov. she is Utah's chief election officer. Former Utah State Senator, SD-7, 2013-2021.

deidre-henderson.notes_set: Utah Lt. Governor (R) since 2021, re-elected 2024; chief election officer; prior State Senator SD-7. [2026-06-17 refinement, state tier] DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (highest-value catch): (1) ELECTION INTEGRITY — as chief election officer she aggressively defends Utah's universal vote-by-mail and machine counting and publicly rebukes her own party's fraud narratives ('fear sells, facts are boring'), calls the claim that hand-counting beats machines 'demonstrably false,' and co-signed a bipartisan pro-vote-by-mail statement with a Democratic Oregon SOS — squarely opposite the rubric's paper-ballot / single-day / anti-mail-in positions (3 FALSEs). (2) MARRIAGE — supported SB296 (2015 'Utah Compromise') adding sexual-orientation/gender-identity as protected classes (with religious exemptions) -> biblical_marriage FALSE on opposing LGBTQ-identity promotion in policy. On life she is self-described 'very pro-life,' co-sponsored an 18-week ban (HB136), and backed the Dobbs ruling -> sanctity_of_life TRUE; she did, however, co-lead a women-senators walkout against a MANDATORY-ultrasound bill and amended out the transvaginal requirement (a method/mandate objection, not an abortion-rights vote). Also a Nikki Haley (not Trump) 2024 endorser. Dimensions without a cited position (family, economics, public justice, border, 2A) left null.

0.note: Self-described 'very pro-life, always vote for pro-life bills'; co-sponsored HB136 (18-week ban); joined Cox's statement wholeheartedly supporting the Dobbs ruling.

4.note: Supported SB296 (2015 'Utah Compromise') adding sexual-orientation and gender-identity as protected classes in housing/employment (with religious exemptions); voted to expand LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: Defends machine counting; called the claim that hand-counting beats machine counting 'demonstrably false' — opposite the hand-counted-paper-ballot position.

2.note: Defends Utah's universal vote-by-mail (>97% of primary voters used mail ballots) rather than single-day in-person voting with narrow absentee.

3.note: Champions mass mail-in voting and recommends drop boxes; co-signed a bipartisan pro-vote-by-mail statement with a Democratic Oregon SOS — opposite opposing mass mail-in/drop boxes.

profile.background: Attorney General of Utah since Jan 2025 (elected Nov 2024, 57.84%). Constitutional/appellate attorney (Sidley Austin; counsel to Sens. Bennett and Hatch); former Utah State Rep (HD-49, 2011-2014) and Utah GOP Chair (2019-2021).

derek-brown.notes_set: Utah Attorney General (R) since Jan 2025. [2026-06-17 refinement, state tier] Consistently conservative AG record built on litigation/coalition actions: defending Utah's abortion law in PPAU v. Utah; litigating against gender-dysphoria-as-disability (Section 504 suit; transgender-inmate case); defending the 'Utah Fits All' ESA voucher in court and prosecuting child online-exploitation (PornHub/Aylo $5M consent order, ICAC); aggressive antitrust (Google Play $700M, Live Nation/Ticketmaster); launched a Federalism & Strategic Litigation Section and won public-lands cases (Kane County, Oklahoma v. EPA); joined the 18-AG amicus supporting the EO ending birthright citizenship and praised the federal immigration crackdown; joined the 26-AG HALT Fentanyl letter. Old 2011-2013 House record is thin/not topic-tagged beyond HB421/SB152. No progressive-side deviation found (bipartisan items are procedural consumer-protection collaborations). Cells without Brown-era cited action (religious-liberty brief, CBDC, election mechanics, 2A action) left null; Reyes-era briefs not attributed to him.

0.note: AG office is actively defending Utah's abortion-restriction law in PPAU v. Utah and moved for summary judgment to reinstate it.

2.note: Litigated against the Section 504 rule adding gender dysphoria as a disability; argued gender dysphoria is not ADA-covered in the transgender-inmate case — affirms biological sex.

0.note: Defending the 'Utah Fits All' ESA scholarship (school-choice voucher) before the Utah Supreme Court; spoke at a school-choice rally.

3.note: Secured a $5M consent order against Aylo/PornHub over CSAM and ran aggressive ICAC prosecutions ('Operation Hive Strike') against child sexual exploitation.

4.note: Aggressive antitrust action against monopolies (Google Play $700M settlement; Live Nation/Ticketmaster monopoly trial win).

0.note: Prosecutorial, tough-on-crime AG; joined the 26-AG HALT Fentanyl letter and secured violent-crime convictions — backs law enforcement.

3.note: Launched a 'Federalism and Strategic Litigation Section' to defend state sovereignty in disputes with the federal government.

4.note: Won public-lands cases (Kane County v. United States rights-of-way; Oklahoma v. EPA) resisting federal control of state land.

1.note: Publicly praised the federal immigration crackdown (border visit) and prosecuted human-smuggling — supports enforcement/removal.

4.note: Joined the 18-AG amicus brief supporting the executive order ending birthright citizenship for children of illegal aliens.
